report_text: Overall medium stability risk: the cleanser should perform well at ambient conditions, with manageable viscosity drift under heat and freeze–thaw if salt and process controls are optimized.

executive_summary.brief_stability_conclusion: The surfactant gel is expected to be physically stable with robust shear-thinning viscosity, but shows moderate risk of viscosity drift and clouding under thermal and freeze–thaw stress.
key_risks_overview: Salt-thickened surfactant system sensitive to NaCl concentration and temperature
PEG-150 Distearate structuring may weaken at elevated temperature (45°C)
Potential cloud point effects from nonionic surfactant (Decyl Glucoside) at low temperature
executive_summary.expected_shelf_life_behavior: At ambient storage, viscosity and clarity should remain acceptable over 12 weeks with minor drift. Accelerated and freeze–thaw conditions may induce reversible viscosity loss or transient haze.

thermal_sensitivity: Viscosity decrease at 45°C due to weakened structuring
ph_sensitivity: pH drift may reduce preservative efficiency and viscosity
electrolyte_sensitivity: Narrow NaCl window critical for viscosity stability
